questions.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = 2x + 3$. Which is the correct $y$-row?
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

options.why: Left out the constant $+3$ and recorded $2x$ only.
Read $2x$ as $2 + x$, adding the coefficient instead of multiplying.
Added $3$ to each $x$ first, then doubled, evaluating $2(x + 3)$.

questions.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = 2x + 5$. Which is the correct $y$-row?
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & -3 & -2 & -1 & 0 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

options.why: Ignored the negative signs on the $x$-values, substituting $3, 2, 1$.
Left out the constant $+5$ and recorded $2x$ only.
Negated the whole result, applying the sign to the $+5$ as well.

questions.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = -2x + 1$. Which is the correct $y$-row?
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

options.why: Ignored the negative sign on the coefficient, using $+2x$.
Negated the whole expression, changing the $+1$ to $-1$.
Read $-2x$ as $-2 + x$, adding $x$ instead of multiplying by $-2$.

questions.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = \frac{x}{2} + 1$. Which is the correct $y$-row?
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 2 & 4 & 6 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

options.why: Multiplied by $2$ instead of dividing by $2$.
Left out the constant $+1$ and recorded $\frac{x}{2}$ only.

questions.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = -2x - 3$. Which is the correct $y$-row?
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & -3 & -1 & 1 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

options.why: Ignored the negative sign on the coefficient, using $+2x$.
Left out the constant $-3$ and recorded $-2x$ only.
Added the constant instead of subtracting, evaluating $-2x + 3$.

questions.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = \frac{x}{2} + 1$. Which is the correct $y$-row?
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & -4 & -2 & 2 & 4 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

options.why: Multiplied by $2$ instead of dividing by $2$.
Left out the constant $+1$ and recorded $\frac{x}{2}$ only.
